commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes cron doctor‚Äôs legacy notify migration to delete top-level `notify: true` when no usable `cron.webhook` target exists, preserving existing delivery, and adds regression tests for `delivery.mode: none` and announce delivery.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +106. Total +128 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main‚Äôs doctor path can be traced from `notifyCount` in `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore` to `migrateLegacyNotifyFallback` warning without setting `changed` when `cron.webhook` is absent, so the same raw config job is detected again on the next run.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Queue a small docs-only repair on this PR because the code fix is bounded and the remaining actionable gap is documentation alignment.

target.title: fix(doctor): drop inert legacy cron notify when cron.webhook is unset
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #44460.

`openclaw doctor --fix` left the legacy top-level `notify: true` cron field in place whenever `cron.webhook` was **unset**, so the dead flag survived every repair. Because the legacy-store preview line and the "Doctor warnings" note are both driven by `rawJobs.filter((job) => job.notify === true)`, each subsequent `doctor` run re-detected the same job and re-emitted the identical `still uses legacy notify: true` preview and warning ‚Äî an endless repair loop the operator can never clear.

The top-level `notify` flag is inert legacy metadata for these jobs: cron delivery is driven entirely by `delivery` (`delivery.mode` / `delivery.channel` / ‚Ä¶), never by the top-level `notify` (there is no runtime read of a top-level cron `job.notify` anywhere under `src/cron/**`). So when there is no migration target, the correct repair is to drop the dead flag, not to warn-and-keep it forever.

## What changed

**`src/commands/doctor/cron/legacy-notify.ts`** ‚Äî `migrateLegacyNotifyFallback` now resolves the two previously-unresolved no-target branches **when `cron.webhook` is unset**:

1. `delivery.mode` is `none` / `webhook` (without a valid target) / absent ‚Üí drop the inert `notify` and mark the store changed.
2. Existing `announce` / legacy-chat delivery ‚Üí drop the inert `notify`; the existing delivery object is left **exactly as-is** (no silent change of delivery semantics).

When `cron.webhook` is **set but not a valid HTTP(S) URL** (e.g. `ftp://‚Ä¶`), the existing actionable warning ("‚Ä¶cron.webhook is not a valid HTTP(S) URL so doctor cannot migrate it automatically") is preserved unchanged, so the operator is still told to fix the URL rather than silently losing migration intent. The valid-webhook migration paths are untouched.

**`src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts`** ‚Äî two regression tests: (a) `delivery.mode: none` + unset `cron.webhook` removes `notify` from the persisted config-JSON layer doctor re-reads, and a second `doctor --fix` no longer emits the legacy-notify preview (loop broken); (b) `announce` delivery + unset `cron.webhook` drops `notify` while leaving the announce delivery unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `openclaw doctor --fix` repeatedly re-warning about ‚Äî and never clearing ‚Äî legacy `notify: true` cron jobs when `cron.webhook` is unset (#44460). After this patch the inert flag is removed once and the repair loop stops.

**Real environment tested:** Local checkout at HEAD `1503931c4d078c2183570892bbd9965892524906` (a worktree of `origin/main`), Node v22.22.0. Drove the real exported `migrateLegacyNotifyFallback` via `node --import tsx` (the actual production code path ‚Äî no mocks/stubs), and the real `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore` doctor entry against a real SQLite-backed cron store in a temp directory.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
# 1) Real production function, before vs after, across every branch shape:
node --import tsx ./harness.mts   # imports & calls migrateLegacyNotifyFallback({ jobs, legacyWebhook })

# 2) End-to-end through the real doctor entry + a real SQLite-backed cron store:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:** Real console output from `node --import tsx` driving the actual exported `migrateLegacyNotifyFallback` (the production function itself, not a mock), with `cron.webhook` unset:
```
BEFORE (origin/main):
  A delivery.mode none         -> changed=false  notify=true (KEPT)  warn "cron.webhook is unset‚Ä¶cannot migrate"
  C announce / chat delivery   -> changed=false  notify=true (KEPT)  warn "cron.webhook is unset‚Ä¶cannot migrate"
  D no delivery                -> changed=false  notify=true (KEPT)  warn "cron.webhook is unset‚Ä¶cannot migrate"

AFTER (this patch):
  A delivery.mode none         -> changed=true   notify REMOVED      delivery {mode:"none"} preserved, no warning
  B delivery.mode webhook(bad) -> changed=true   notify REMOVED      delivery {mode:"webhook",to:"ftp://x/y"} preserved
  C announce / chat delivery   -> changed=true   notify REMOVED      delivery {to:"telegram:123"} preserved
  D no delivery                -> changed=true   notify REMOVED

Controls (behavior intentionally unchanged):
  E cron.webhook set but "ftp://‚Ä¶" (invalid) -> changed=false notify kept warn "‚Ä¶not a valid HTTP(S) URL‚Ä¶"
  F delivery webhook + valid to              -> changed=true  migrated
  G delivery none + valid cron.webhook        -> changed=true  migrated to webhook
```

**Observed result after fix:** With `cron.webhook` unset the inert `notify` flag is removed once (`changed=true` ‚Üí the store is re-saved); a second real `doctor --fix` pass then reads `notifyCount === 0`, so the legacy-notify preview line and the "Doctor warnings" note are both gone ‚Äî the loop the issue describes is broken. The existing delivery (including `announce`) is byte-for-byte preserved. A configured-but-invalid `cron.webhook` still surfaces the actionable "not a valid HTTP(S) URL" warning.

**What was not tested:** No end-to-end live scheduler dispatching an actual cron notification ‚Äî delivery dispatch is unchanged by this patch; only the inert doctor-migration metadata is touched. No non-loopback / TLS webhook targets ‚Äî out of scope; valid-webhook migration paths are unchanged.

## Validation (supplemental)

- End-to-end regression through the real doctor entry + real SQLite store: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts` ‚Üí `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 26 passed (26)` (includes the 2 new #44460 regression tests: config-JSON-layer notify removal + second-pass no-loop, and announce-preservation).
- `tsgo:core` and `tsgo:core:test` typecheck ‚Üí exit 0. `git diff --check` clean. `oxlint` clean on changed files.
target.author: alix-007
